name: getZHR
description: Returns the current Zenith Hourly Rate (ZHR) for sporadic meteors. ZHR represents the number of meteors a single observer would see per hour under ideal conditions (dark sky, radiant at zenith).

compatibility.notes: Introduced in Stellarium 0.15.0, works on all later versions

name: setZHR
description: Sets the Zenith Hourly Rate (ZHR) for sporadic meteors. Higher values increase the number of visible meteors. Typical background sporadic rates range from 5 to 15 meteors per hour.

parameters.name: zhr
parameters.description: Zenith Hourly Rate (meteors per hour). Recommended range: 1 to 100.

name: getFlagShow
description: Returns whether sporadic meteor rendering is currently enabled. When disabled, no sporadic meteors will be displayed regardless of ZHR settings.
returnDescription: true if sporadic meteors are visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagShow
description: Shows or hides sporadic meteors (background meteors not associated with any meteor shower). When disabled, no sporadic meteors will appear even if ZHR is set to a positive value.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: true to show sporadic meteors, false to hide them

name: setMaxVelocity
description: Sets the maximum velocity for sporadic meteors in kilometers per second. Meteor velocities typically range from 11 km/s (slow) to 72 km/s (very fast). Higher velocities produce longer, faster streaks.

parameters.name: maxVelocity
parameters.description: Maximum meteor velocity in km/s (recommended range: 11 to 72)

name: getFlagForcedMeteorsActivity
description: Returns whether forced meteor activity is enabled. When enabled, meteors will be displayed even when the atmosphere is disabled. Useful for planetarium shows where atmosphere might be turned off.
returnDescription: true if meteors show when atmosphere is disabled, false otherwise

name: setFlagForcedMeteorsActivity
description: Enables or disables forced meteor activity. When enabled, sporadic meteors will be displayed even when the atmosphere is turned off. This is particularly useful for planetarium environments where the atmosphere may be disabled for technical reasons.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: true to enable meteors when atmosphere is disabled, false to require atmosphere
